back_translation: 'Call out now—is there anyone who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?
For resentment kills a fool, and envy slays the simple.
I have seen a fool taking root, but suddenly his dwelling was cursed.
His children are far from safety, crushed at the gate with no one to deliver them.
The hungry eat his harvest, taking it even from among the thorns, and the thirsty pant after his wealth.
For affliction does not come up from the dust, and trouble does not sprout from the ground.
But man is born to trouble, as the sparks fly upward.
But as for me, I would seek God, and lay my cause before God,
who does great things beyond searching out, and wonders without number;
who gives rain on the earth and sends water upon the fields;
who sets the lowly on high, and lifts mourners to safety.
He frustrates the schemes of the crafty, so that their hands accomplish nothing.
He catches the wise in their own craftiness, and the plans of the cunning quickly fail.
By day they meet with darkness, and at noon they grope as in the night.
But He saves the needy from the sword of their mouth, and from the hand of the powerful.
So the poor have hope, and injustice must shut its mouth.
Behold, blessed is the man whom God corrects; so do not despise the discipline of the Almighty.
For He wounds, but He also binds up; He strikes, but His hands also heal.
He will deliver you from six troubles, and in seven no harm will touch you.
In famine He will redeem you from death, and in war from the power of the sword.
You will be hidden from the scourge of the tongue, and will not fear destruction when it comes.
You will laugh at destruction and famine, and need not fear the beasts of the earth.
For you will have a covenant with the stones of the field, and the beasts of the field will be at peace with you.
You will know that your tent is safe, and when you inspect your dwelling, you will find nothing missing.
You will know that your offspring will be many, and your descendants like the grass of the earth.
You will come to the grave in full old age, like a sheaf of grain gathered in its season.
Behold, we have examined this, and it is true; hear it, and know it for yourself.'
